title: Executive Order 9437—Revocation of Executive Order No. 9165, Providing for the Protection of Essential Facilities from Sabotage and Other Destructive Acts

flags.justification: A pure revocation; no new decision-making authority is created for anyone.
No ongoing program is created by a revocation for responsibility to become unclear about.
Single purpose: revoking the named prior order.
A revocation removes a compliance regime rather than creating a new one.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A one-time revocation grants no ongoing authority requiring reauthorization.

The order explicitly preserves substance while revoking form: it 'shall not be deemed to terminate any facility security functions of the War Department nor to preclude any necessary cooperative arrangement' with the agencies named in the revoked order — protective language, not a removal of oversight.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A housekeeping revocation with explicit continuity language for the underlying protective function; minimal second-order effect.
The order explicitly preserves 'necessary cooperative arrangement' with the agencies named in the revoked order — careful preservation, not redirection.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
